user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
1) Reversion trading is the hardest thing to do in the market.
with 0DTEs you literally need to have exact timing with the best vol profile in order to catch alpha. 
If you understand the probability distirbution, you can tell, that in most of the times, we can read the market from positioning and all the metrics we have, but there are lower/decreasing probability events that have higher negative or positive impacts (depending on if it is on the left or on the right side of the bell curve), and there the predictive models start to be inaccurate. Not necessary 100% inaccurate... but the point is that you have less and less metrics that can foreshadow the lower prob momentums
comment.raw_body:

Not necessary 100% inaccurate... but the point is that you have less and less metrics that can foreshadow the lower prob momentums

comment.body:
We have a big advantage. And this is that I figured it out what this tape is all about, what is the story. 
As I shared with you after OpEx, the first Monday, we gonna see this steady slow decline.
You ask: -slow? , i say: yes, slow, look, we are like ~200 pts down from the ATH, but it took one week. It is okay, this is not a big flush that happens in 1-2 days like a left tail event. 
I explained what is the key driver behind this: gov wants lower tech and lower oil, also wants to checkmate the Fed to trigger QE, they need room. 
I keep telling it basically since last december, that they will do this, before a steady growth can be faked

comment.body:
what i usually do when i trade manually is that I decide what side I wanna trade that day: put or call.
and i mark my potential targets, and describe to myself what are the flows that i wanna see in the metrics in order to jump into the trade.
This is what I basically do for you with my intraday posts. 
If I don't see the setup giving it to me, I simply stay out, and don't take any positions... never chasing

comment.body: and when you see a deviation from what the positioning would indicate, i.e. vol decreaseing when it should increase... or a certain long gamma lvl triggered no reaction while the vol was relatively low (so the gamma lvl should have had an effect on the momentum, but it haven't), you can tell that some anomaly, some outside force is driving the market
